title: Vaccinium macilentum
taxonomy: Vaccinium macilentum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It belongs to the genus Vaccinium, which includes blueberries, cranberries, and other similar plants. The exact classification of Vaccinium macilentum may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a member of the order Ericales. This order includes a wide range of flowering plants, many of which are found in temperate and boreal regions. Vaccinium macilentum is likely to be further classified at the species level based on its unique characteristics and genetic makeup.

intro: Vaccinium macilentum, also known as the creeping bilberry or slender bilberry, is a low-growing, woody shrub native to Europe, known for its dark blue berries and leaves. The health benefits of Vaccinium macilentum include its potential to act as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent, which may help to protect against oxidative stress, inflammation, and infection. The bioactive constituents of Vaccinium macilentum include anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Vaccinium macilentum is often used in traditional medicine and is commonly prepared as a dried leaf or berry extract, tea, or tincture, which can be used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall health and well-being.
